bead.title: bench: ./benchmark shell driver
bead.description:
Replace cmd/bench sweep execution with a thin scripts/benchmark/benchmark shell driver. Per the 2026-05-15 plan, execution moves out of Go to a shell script that invokes fiz directly (or Harbor + FizeauAgent for TerminalBench cells). cmd/bench survives only as analytics tooling (aggregate, import-evidence, validate).

Operator contract:
  ./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B           # run
  ./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --plan    # validate + print matrix, no side effects
  ./benchmark validate                            # fast offline validation
  ./benchmark preflight --profile P               # endpoint probe (explicit)
  ./benchmark profiles                            # list
  ./benchmark bench-sets                          # list

Rules (from spec):
1. --plan is pure: no file writes, no Docker pulls, no preflight, no benchmark-results/ dir creation.
2. Cell directory created only when cell is about to start.
3. --profile and --bench-set both required (no implicit defaults).
4. validate is fast and offline (schema + cross-reference only).
5. preflight is the only command that probes endpoints.
6. Manifest reading: one Go helper (e.g. 'fiz-bench plan --json' or 'fiz-bench manifest --json') emits JSON; shell consumes JSON, never YAML.

Concurrency: shell reads profile.concurrency_group, looks up scripts/benchmark/concurrency-groups.yaml, enforces semaphore via GNU parallel or background-job counter.

TerminalBench cells: shell invokes Harbor with FizeauAgent (scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py), forwarding profile-derived env. Simple prompt cells: shell invokes fiz directly with profile-derived flags.

Resume: 'cell exists with terminal final_status in {pass, fail, timeout} -> skip' unless --force-rerun. 'final_status == invalid' -> skip unless --retry-invalid.

Old fiz-bench sweep execution path continues to work until PR 4.

In-scope files:
- scripts/benchmark/benchmark (new shell script)
- cmd/bench/plan.go or cmd/bench/manifest.go (new Go helper that emits JSON)
- cmd/bench/main.go (register the new subcommand)
- cmd/bench/testdata/plans/ (golden plan fixtures for regression guard)
- cmd/bench/sweep_test.go or new plan_test.go (parity fixtures)

Out-of-scope:
- Deleting cmd/bench/lanes*.go or sweep.go (PR 4)
- Backfill of existing cells (PR 2)
- Analytics switch (PR 3)

Spec: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification.md section 'PR 1d' and 'Regression Guardrails'
bead.acceptance:
1. 'bash -n scripts/benchmark/benchmark' passes shellcheck syntax.
2. './scripts/benchmark/benchmark --plan --profile vidar-ds4 --bench-set tb-2-1-timing-baseline' prints a matrix to stdout and creates zero files (verified by 'find benchmark-results -newer /tmp/before' returning empty after running --plan).
3. './scripts/benchmark/benchmark profiles' lists every profile in scripts/benchmark/profiles/.
4. './scripts/benchmark/benchmark bench-sets' lists every bench-set in scripts/benchmark/bench-sets/.
5. Golden plan fixtures at cmd/bench/testdata/plans/ exist for at least: profile=vidar-ds4 bench-set=tb-2-1-timing-baseline; profile=sindri-llamacpp,vidar-ds4 bench-set=tb-2-1-or-passing.
6. 'go test ./cmd/bench/...' passes including new plan parity tests.
